hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric fit in the SSTR2-unique ECL3 (284/286) pocket. These changes should increase selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that the lack of selectivity is due to conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions with SSTR3/SSTR5. The next iteration will focus on disrupting these interactions through targeted mutations while preserving the pharmacophore.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must target non-pharmacophore positions that are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. These positions are likely forming conserved-pocket contacts that reduce selectivity.

These mutations are selected to disrupt conserved hydrophobic or hydrogen-bonding interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining or improving SSTR2 binding. For example, introducing charged residues at positions 1 and 2 may break off-target salt bridges.
